title: Verify Operator Station Stacklight Locations
summary: Use this reference procedure to confirm that the operator station stacklights are located as documented in the manual: two stacklights mounted on the top of the frame on either side of the chute.
when_to_use: Use when identifying operator station components or verifying that the stacklights are physically located at the operator station as documented in the manual and Figure 3-1/Table 3-1 references.
not_for: Do not use this procedure to interpret stacklight colors, statuses, or alarms.
Do not use this procedure for corrective action if a stacklight is missing, relocated, or malfunctioning.
Do not use this procedure for hospital station stacklight inspection.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Visually inspect the operator station and compare observed stacklight locations to the documented manual reference.

safety_notes: This source supports visual location verification only.
No source-supported lockout, shutdown, or intervention steps are provided in this procedure.
access_or_tools_needed: Visual access to the operator station
Figure 3-1 Operator Station (Panels Removed for Clarity)
Operator station feature list in Table 3-1

access_required: Access to view the operator station
system_preconditions: Operator station must be physically visible so the chute and top frame area can be inspected.
external_references: Figure 3-1 Operator Station (Panels Removed for Clarity)

visual_references.description: Reference photo of the operator station with labeled components, including the chute and stacklight location context.

steps.title: Go to the operator station and locate the chute
steps.purpose: Establish the correct physical reference point for locating the documented stacklights.
steps.instruction: Go to the operator station and identify the chute in the operator station area.
steps.expected_result: The chute is identified at the operator station.
steps.healthy_condition: The chute can be visually located as part of the operator station layout.
steps.failure_condition: The chute cannot be confidently identified from the operator station view.

actions.label: Locate operator station chute
actions.exact_action: Go to the operator station and locate the chute.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the operator station reference image to identify the chute within the operator-facing component layout.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the operator station layout does not allow the chute to be identified.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The operator station is the area where an operator receives parcels from the tippers. It includes two tippers, a chute, plastic curtains, rotary station, HMI screen, and four push-buttons. Two stacklights are mounted on the top of the frame on either side of the chute.
Figure 3-1 provides a reference view of the operator station with labeled features.

steps.title: Inspect the top of the frame around the chute area
steps.purpose: Direct the inspection to the documented mounting area for the stacklights.
steps.instruction: Look at the top of the frame around the chute area.
steps.expected_result: The top frame area around the chute is visible for comparison to the documented stacklight location.
steps.healthy_condition: The top of the frame around the chute can be clearly observed.
steps.failure_condition: The top frame area around the chute cannot be clearly observed.

actions.label: Inspect top frame area
actions.exact_action: Look at the top of the frame around the chute area.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look at the top of the frame in relation to the chute to orient the expected stacklight mounting area.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the top of the frame around the chute cannot be visually inspected.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Two stacklights are mounted on the top of the frame on either side of the chute.
Figure 3-1 provides a reference view of the operator station with labeled features.

steps.title: Verify two stacklights are mounted on either side of the chute
steps.purpose: Confirm the documented quantity and relative position of the operator station stacklights.
steps.instruction: Identify whether there are two stacklights mounted on the top of the frame, one on either side of the chute.
steps.expected_result: Two stacklights are observed on the top of the frame, one on each side of the chute.
steps.healthy_condition: The observed stacklight positions match the documented location: two stacklights on the top of the frame on either side of the chute.
steps.failure_condition: One or both stacklights are missing, relocated, or not positioned on either side of the chute as documented.

actions.label: Verify stacklight count and position
actions.exact_action: Identify whether there are two stacklights mounted on the top of the frame, one on either side of the chute.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use Figure 3-1 to compare the observed stacklight positions relative to the chute and top frame.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the stacklights are missing, relocated, or do not match the documented position.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Two stacklights are mounted on the top of the frame on either side of the chute.
Figure 3-1 provides a reference view of the operator station with labeled features.

steps.title: Compare the observation to the documented feature references
steps.purpose: Validate the observed stacklight location against the manual's operator station feature list and figure reference.
steps.instruction: Compare the observed stacklight positions to the documented operator station feature list and figure reference.
steps.expected_result: The observed stacklight locations align with the operator station documentation in Table 3-1 and Figure 3-1.
steps.healthy_condition: The observed stacklights match the documented operator station feature reference and figure-based layout.
steps.failure_condition: The observed stacklight locations do not match the documented operator station feature reference and figure-based layout.

actions.label: Compare to manual references
actions.exact_action: Compare the observed stacklight positions to the documented operator station feature list and figure reference.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the labeled operator station photo as the primary visual reference for stacklight placement.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed stacklight locations do not match the documented position.
Escalate if additional interpretation is needed beyond location verification.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Table 3-1 lists Stacklight as an operator station feature.
Figure 3-1 provides a reference view of the operator station with labeled features.
Table 3-1 identifies the operator station features as: 1 Tipper, 2 Chute, 3 Stacklight, 4 Plastic curtain, 5 Operator station HMI, 6 Push-buttons, 7 Rotary station.

success_criteria: The user confirms that two stacklights are mounted on the top of the frame on either side of the chute.
The observed stacklight positions match the operator station documentation in Figure 3-1 and Table 3-1.
healthy_conditions: Both stacklights are mounted on the top of the frame.
The stacklights are positioned one on either side of the chute.
The observed layout matches the operator station feature references.
failure_conditions: The stacklights are relocated from the documented position.
The observed stacklight arrangement does not match the documented operator station layout.
The source does not provide status meanings or corrective actions for stacklight issues.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the stacklights are missing, relocated, or do not match the documented position.
Escalate if the user needs stacklight status meanings, alarm interpretation, or corrective actions, because this source supports location verification only.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Operator station reference image showing the chute and surrounding frame area used to verify stacklight placement.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Two stacklights are mounted on the top of the frame on either side of the chute.
Table 3-1 lists Stacklight as an operator station feature.
Figure 3-1 provides a reference view of the operator station with labeled features.

missing_details: The source does not provide stacklight color meanings, status interpretation, or alarm behavior for the operator station.
The source does not provide corrective actions if stacklights are missing or relocated.
The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source does not state whether production stop or LOTO is required for this visual verification.
